Details: Removal of Cryptosporidum in water by conventional treatment (coagulation, clarification and filtration) is effective, with removals as high as 6 logs reported. LT1 cites effective filtration, with sufficiently low turbidities, as means of Cryptosporidium removal. It is highly dependent on source water quality, chemical pretreatment as well as filtration rate, bed depth and media types. The coagulant dose is an important parameter in removal. Turbidity and natural organic matter removal have been shown to improve pathogen removal.
